title: Add Numbers in Standard Form
description: Add multiple quantities written in standard (non-scientific) notation.
condition: Given two or more numbers in standard form to combine into a total.
response: Compute their arithmetic sum.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Used after converting from scientific notation where needed.

title: Add Numbers with Same Power of 10
description: Add numbers written with the same power of 10 by adding coefficients and keeping the common power of 10.
condition: Two or more numbers written as a×10^n and b×10^n with the same exponent n.
response: Compute (a+b)×10^n.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare Coefficients with Same Power of 10
description: When two quantities are expressed with the same power of 10, compare their coefficients to determine which is larger and by what factor.
condition: Two numbers written in scientific notation with identical powers of 10 (e.g., a·10^n and b·10^n).
response: Compare coefficients a and b; determine larger value and compute multiplicative factor a/b or b/a.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $10 \cdot 10^6$ and $2 \cdot 10^6$: since $10/2 = 5$, the first is 5 times larger.

title: Compute Time from Unit Rate
description: Given a growth rate (units per second) and a target amount, compute time by dividing amount by rate.
condition: A constant rate r (units per second) and a target quantity Q are given.
response: Compute time as $t = Q / r$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If growth rate is $10^{-9}$ m/s, time for 1 m is $1 \div 10^{-9} = 10^9$ seconds.

title: Convert Meters to Centimeters Using Powers of 10
description: Recognize that 1 meter equals 100 centimeters and express this as a power of 10 for use in calculations.
condition: Need to convert between meters and centimeters.
response: Use $1\text{ m} = 100\text{ cm} = 10^2\text{ cm}$ (or divide by $10^2$ to convert cm to m).
label: Fact

title: Convert Scientific Notation to Standard Form
description: Rewrite a number from scientific notation into standard decimal form.
condition: A number written as $a \times 10^n$.
response: Move the decimal point in $a$ right if $n>0$ or left if $n<0$ by $|n|$ places.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Standard Form to Scientific Notation
description: Rewrite a number in standard form as a number between 1 and 10 multiplied by a power of 10.
condition: A number in standard form (e.g., 680,000 or 600,000,000).
response: Express as a×10^n where 1≤a<10.
label: Principle / Model

title: Determine Multiplicative Comparison
description: Find how many times larger one quantity is than another by forming and evaluating a ratio.
condition: Two positive quantities are given and comparison 'how many times larger' is requested.
response: Compute ratio (larger ÷ smaller) to find multiplicative factor.
label: Principle / Model
example: If Canada is $10 \cdot 10^6$ km$^2$ and Mexico is $2 \cdot 10^6$ km$^2$, then $\frac{10}{2} = 5$, so Canada is 5 times larger.

title: Divide Powers of 10
description: When dividing powers of 10 with the same base, subtract exponents.
condition: Expression of the form $10^a / 10^b$.
response: Compute $10^{a-b}$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Divide Powers with Same Base
description: When dividing powers with the same base, subtract the exponents.
condition: Expression of the form a^m / a^n with same base a.
response: Rewrite as a^(m−n).
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate Power of 10
description: Convert a power of 10 into its standard decimal form.
condition: Expression of the form 10^n where n is an integer.
response: Write the decimal value with n places (positive n: 1 followed by n zeros; negative n: decimal with |n| places).
label: Principle

title: Expand Exponent as Repeated Multiplication
description: Interpret a power as repeated multiplication of its base.
condition: An expression of the form a^n with n a positive integer.
response: Rewrite as a · a · ... · a (n factors).
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Calculator Scientific Notation (E Notation)
description: Recognize calculator output using E notation as scientific notation.
condition: A number written in the form $aE n$ (e.g., 7.3E4).
response: Interpret as $a \times 10^n$ and classify as scientific notation.
label: Concept

title: Identify Equivalent Exponential Expressions
description: Determine whether a given expression is equivalent in value to a target exponential expression.
condition: A target exponential expression and a candidate expression involving exponents.
response: Apply exponent rules or simplification to decide if both expressions have equal value.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Valid Scientific Notation
description: Determine whether an expression is correctly written in scientific notation.
condition: An expression of the form $a \times 10^n$.
response: Confirm it is scientific notation if $1 \le a < 10$ and $n$ is an integer; otherwise reject.
label: Concept

title: Interpret Scaled Number Line with Powers of 10
description: Determine a value represented on a number line scaled by powers of 10 using tick mark intervals.
condition: A point located between labeled powers of 10 with equal subdivisions.
response: Identify the interval value and compute the point’s value accordingly.
label: Production / Skill
example: Between $4\times10^6$ and $5\times10^6$ with 10 equal parts, the second tick is $4.2\times10^6$.

title: Multiply by Decimal Equivalent of Percent
description: Apply a percent increase or decrease by multiplying by its decimal equivalent.
condition: A quantity and a percent change p%.
response: Multiply the quantity by (1±p/100) or by p/100 for the change amount.
label: Principle / Model

title: Multiply Numbers in Scientific Notation
description: Multiply coefficients and add exponents when multiplying numbers in scientific notation.
condition: Two numbers written as a×10^m and b×10^n being multiplied.
response: Compute (a·b)×10^(m+n), then adjust to proper scientific notation if needed.
label: Principle / Model

title: Multiply Powers with Same Base
description: When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents.
condition: Expression of the form a^m · a^n with same base a.
response: Rewrite as a^(m+n).
label: Principle / Model

title: Negative Exponent as Reciprocal
description: A negative exponent indicates the reciprocal of the corresponding positive power.
condition: Expression of the form a^{-n}, where a ≠ 0.
response: Rewrite as 1 / a^n.
label: Principle

title: Power of a Power Rule
description: When raising a power to a power, multiply the exponents.
condition: Expression of the form (a^m)^n.
response: Rewrite as a^(m·n).
label: Principle

title: Power of a Product Rule
description: When two factors share the same exponent, combine them into a single power with multiplied bases.
condition: An expression of the form a^n · b^n with the same exponent n.
response: Rewrite as (ab)^n.
label: Principle / Model

title: Recognize When Bases Cannot Be Combined
description: Understand that powers with different bases and unequal exponents cannot be rewritten as a single power without changing structure.
condition: An expression of the form a^m · b^n where a ≠ b and m ≠ n.
response: Conclude that it cannot be written as a single exponent using standard exponent rules.
label: Concept
example: $3^{4} \cdot 8^{3}$ cannot be written as $(3 \cdot 8)^{k}$ because the exponents differ (4 ≠ 3).

title: Rewrite Standard Form as Power of 10
description: Express a whole number as a coefficient times a power of 10.
condition: A large whole number such as 10,000,000 is given.
response: Rewrite as $a \cdot 10^n$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Zero Exponent Rule
description: Any nonzero base raised to the zero power equals 1.
condition: An expression of the form a^0 where a ≠ 0.
response: Replace with 1.
label: Concept
